# Transcrição: 02-Networking at Live Events

- **Fonte:** 02-Networking at Live Events
- **Duração:** 04:43
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

There are so many valuable ways to make connections, especially in person. Because you don't want to just rely solely on your warm network for sales, you're going to run out of people in your contacts list. It doesn't matter how many friends or colleagues or people that you know, it's that you need to be in the mindset that you are consistently looking out for ways to form new connections and meet new people. The key is making these connections in person. You're probably feeling a little nervous when we talk about doing things in person. That's totally normal. It can be scary approaching strangers. But in order to set you up for success, I want you to think of a couple of things. Where can you find these connections? It's important that you know the people that you are leaning into. Where are they hanging out? I want you to pretend for a moment that you are your ideal client. Ask yourself, what events are you going to? What things am I already paying for that I could attend? Where would I want to go? As you answer these questions, you'll narrow down your ideal client and where they might be hanging out. For example, if you have a course that helps dog owners fix behavioral issues, think of places that dog owners are likely to go. A good place is a dog park. This is where you meet the dog owners who might be actually struggling with some of the pain points that you teach around. Here's just a couple of other places that you might be able to make these connections. Maybe it's the seminars that you love going to. You can go on meetup.com and find places to meet up with people of similar interest as you. You can even join your local Chamber of Commerce. Look at the events that your city is throwing. Join the local Facebook groups and see what's going on in your neighborhood. Always be on the lookout for new places where you could meet new people. You have a bunch of ideas of where you could meet some people now. So how do you approach them? Most important rule, be genuine. Be authentically you. If you don't want to come across as delzy or weird, just be genuine. People respond well when they feel that you care and that you're showing up as your authentic self. This is going to help you build rapport. And it's important that you build rapport because you don't want to jump into the sale right away. You need to get to know them. I want you to really ask them questions and listen to their answers. This is normal human behavior of getting to know someone. People love to talk about themselves. So ask the questions. When you're listening to the answers thoughtfully, you're going to be showing them that you care. And you're going to ask them follow up questions.

## [03:00 - 04:43]

This way you can start to ask about any potential pains that they might be encountering that you can have solutions for. So let's talk about the dog behavior example from earlier. Ask them which dog is theirs. How long have they had their dog? As you listen carefully, you might notice that they mentioned their dog as a high energy breed. You can then ask them more leading questions like if they've ever found it difficult to train their dog. When you lead with value, you have the experience in your niche. Let them see that experience. If they mention a problem or a pain point, give them some helpful advice. For example, if the dog owner that you had just talked to tells you that their dog choose everything in sight, help them understand why before giving them a quick tip. This lets them see your expertise as they are engaging in the conversation and appreciating your advice. You can then tell them about your course or your coaching. On the worksheet provided in your resources below, you're going to see that there is an opportunity for you to pretend that you're your ideal client. I want you to brainstorm as many places as possible where they might go. Try to come up with a variety of options, public places, free events, paid events, anywhere that you can see that you can connect with them in person. It's important that you are approaching people and that you are approachable at the same time. You must be open to discuss things. It's an easy, easy way to position yourself as someone who's passionate about your niche. Get out there and start meeting some new people to add to your network.
